Class ProcessInstanceService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.process.ProcessInstanceService
@Service public class ProcessInstanceService extends Object
Starts BPMN process instances for a project or one of its tasks — the point where the engine actually begins to run something.

Orchestration, not ownership. A process instance never owns a task's lifecycle: it creates and coordinates work, while Prioritize stays the system of record for status, time tracking and everything else. The whole coupling is one string,

invalid reference 
Task#getProcessInstanceId()
, plus the instance's business key in the other direction. Remove Flowable and the core still works.

Authorization is project membership (requireMemberOrManager, the same rule as creating a task): whoever may work on a project may start a process on it. There is deliberately no second permission on top — which definitions can run at all is already decided by the activation right in ProcessDefinitionService, and a definition that nobody activated cannot be started here.

Only registered definitions can be started. Definitions deployed straight from the classpath trusted root are visible to the engine but not to this service; that is what makes them the break-glass path rather than a second public API (see ProcessDefinitionStartupReport).

Author:
peter haller

  • Constructor Details

    • ProcessInstanceService

      public ProcessInstanceService(org.flowable.engine.RuntimeService runtimeService, org.flowable.engine.HistoryService historyService, ProcessDefinitionRepository definitionRepository, ProjectService projectService, TaskRepository taskRepository)

  • Method Details

    • businessKeyForProject

      public static String businessKeyForProject(Long projectId)
      The business key of an instance started for a project. One rule, derived, never supplied by the caller: the business key answers "what is this instance about", and that must not be up for negotiation. Event correlation gets its own rule later — this one is not overloaded with it.

    • businessKeyForTask

      public static String businessKeyForTask(Long taskId)
      The business key of an instance started for a task. See businessKeyForProject(Long).

    • startForProject

      @Transactional public ProcessInstanceDTO startForProject(Long projectId, Long definitionId, Map<String,Object> variables, PUser user)
      Starts an instance of an active definition for a whole project.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the project the instance belongs to
      definitionId - the registered definition to start
      variables - optional initial process variables; may be null
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the started instance
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user is not on the project
      NoSuchElementException - if project or definition do not exist
      IllegalStateException - if the definition is not active

    • startForTask

      @Transactional public ProcessInstanceDTO startForTask(Long taskId, Long definitionId, Map<String,Object> variables, PUser user)
      Starts an instance of an active definition for a single task and links the two.

      A task can only carry one instance at a time: starting a second one while the first is still running would overwrite the link and leave the running instance unattributable. Once the linked instance has ended, the task can be started into a new one.

      Parameters:
      taskId - the task the instance belongs to
      definitionId - the registered definition to start
      variables - optional initial process variables; may be null
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member of the task's project)
      Returns:
      the started instance
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the definition is not active, or the task already runs one

    • getForProject

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<ProcessInstanceDTO> getForProject(Long projectId, PUser user)
      The process instances belonging to a project — the ones started for the project itself plus the ones linked to its tasks, running and already finished.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the project
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the instances, empty if the project never started one

    • getForTask

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Optional<ProcessInstanceDTO> getForTask(Long taskId, PUser user)
      The instance a task is linked to, running or finished.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member of the task's project)
      Returns:
      the linked instance, or empty if the task never ran one

    • get

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public ProcessInstanceDTO get(String processInstanceId, PUser user)
      A single instance by its engine id.
      Parameters:
      processInstanceId - the engine's instance id
      user - the requesting user (must be on the owning project)
      Returns:
      the instance
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if the engine does not know it, or the platform did not start it

    • cancel

      @Transactional public void cancel(String processInstanceId, String reason, PUser user)
      Cancels a running instance. The engine deletes its runtime state and keeps the cancellation, with the given reason, in its history — so what happened stays readable afterwards.

      Manager only. Starting a process is everyday work on a project, cutting one short is not: a half-finished process may leave tasks behind that nobody will pick up again. The task's link is deliberately not cleared — it still points at what happened; the task simply becomes startable again because the instance no longer runs.

      Parameters:
      processInstanceId - the engine's instance id
      reason - why it was cancelled; kept in the engine's history
      user - the requesting user (must be the owning project's manager)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the instance has already finished
